review_scope: 17 [Rev 1] Figma frames — Smishing section landing, campaign creation wizard (entry modals + 5 wizard steps including rebuilt Notifications step and completed Review summary), template preview panel, bulk action component, and campaign status screens (Scheduled, In Progress, Result/Completed). Third review pass — direct Figma fixes applied during this pass.
quality_bar_assessment: All S4 and S2 findings resolved across three review passes and a direct-fix session. The Review screen campaign summary, Notifications step form, compliance gate, campaign table, and status screens now form a coherent, trust-correct feature. Two S3 items remain open by design (Reported signal pending PM scope, Preview panel CTA). One S2 accessibility annotation is needed before the dev-handoff doc is written. Design is ready for handoff pending those two pre-handoff tasks.
strengths: Review screen campaign summary (Campaign Setup / Template / Target Audience / Notifications as read-only rows) is the correct pattern for a high-stakes wizard confirmation step
Graduated stat funnel with absolute numbers and percentages remains the strongest individual surface — tells the full campaign story
Compliance acknowledgment correctly implemented as DS Checkbox with disabled CTA state and checked-state annotation
Campaign table landing is production-ready with 5 status types, 7 columns, filter toolbar, and representative data
Notifications step rebuild correctly mirrors the Scheduled detail screen — wizard form and read-only detail view are in sync

findings.location: In Progress and Result screens → graduated stat funnel
findings.issue: The graduated funnel does not include a Reported signal card. PRD §11 defines smishing_reported as a distinct positive risk signal with dedicated event type and scoring weight.
findings.why_it_matters: The Reported signal is the only positive-direction event in the funnel. Without it, admins cannot measure reporting rate, compare it across campaigns, or see the Remediation Agent positive reinforcement trigger in the UI.
findings.recommended_fix: Add a Reported stat card with positive visual treatment. Confirm v1 scope with PM before designing — reporting mechanism is pending Phase 0 validation.
findings.teaching_note: A missing funnel step creates an analytical gap. Funnel design must be driven by the event taxonomy. If PM confirms v1 scope, this is a 30-minute addition.

findings.issue: The Template Preview panel is titled Preview Attack but its primary CTA is Save Changes. The panel contains only informational content with no editing controls.
findings.why_it_matters: Save Changes on a view-only surface implies a phantom commitment. Admins may skip previewing templates to avoid unintended changes.
findings.recommended_fix: Replace Save Changes with Close (if view-only) or Use Template (if the panel triggers template selection). Align Cancel label to match.
principles: Match between system and the real world
findings.teaching_note: CTA copy on a preview panel is a trust signal. The label must accurately describe the state change it causes — or the absence of one.

findings.location: Create New Campaign wizard — Steps Container component
findings.issue: The wizard step bar has no design annotation specifying ARIA roles, step state labels (current / completed / locked), or back-navigation clickability.
findings.why_it_matters: Without the annotation, a developer implementing the wizard has no spec for keyboard accessibility or screen reader behavior on the step progress bar.
findings.recommended_fix: Add a Figma annotation specifying role=navigation + aria-label, each step's current/completed/locked state, and whether completed steps are clickable. Approximately 10 minutes of annotation work.
findings.teaching_note: Wizard step bars are accessibility-critical components. Annotating at design time costs 10 minutes; retrofitting post-implementation costs a sprint.

findings.location: Scheduled and In Progress screens → Templates / Training section
findings.issue: Multiple identical Ransomware 4mins training cards with no title, duration, or category variation.
findings.why_it_matters: Identical placeholder content cannot validate truncation, badge alignment, or layout against real data variation.
findings.recommended_fix: Use varied representative content — at least two different titles, a range of durations, and different category badges.
findings.teaching_note: Placeholder repetition hides layout fragility. Use varied content to stress-test components at design time.

resolved_findings.resolution: Notifications step rebuilt with Training Assignment card and Notifications Config card (Rev 1)
PLAYGROUND sidebar label replaced with Simulations across all frames (Rev 1)
Compliance checkbox replaced with DS Checkbox component + disabled CTA state (Rev 1)
Coming Soon and Secondary placeholder text replaced in stat cards (Rev 1)
Result screen stats corrected to Did Not Engage 89.3% with delta label (Rev 1)
Campaign list table added with 5 representative rows and toolbar (Rev 1)

Review screen Form Container rebuilt with 4-section campaign summary: Campaign Setup (name, channel, schedule, duration), Template (templates, persona, kill chain), Target Audience (users, groups, exclusions), Notifications (trigger, recipients, event). Compliance gate remains below summary (direct fix)
Section header Moderate badges updated to Risk: Moderate across 18 direct-node instances in In Progress and Result screens (direct fix)
3-day accuracy note on Result screen updated to past tense referencing did not engage metric name (direct fix)
Tab 1 placeholder labels replaced with Risk Scores, Training Progress, Time on Platform, Simulation History in both In Progress and Result screens (direct fix)
Log In instance default text replaced with em-dash in In Progress and Result screens (direct fix)

open_questions: [PM] Is the Reported signal card confirmed for v1? Pending Phase 0 validation — answer determines whether DR-05 must be designed before handoff.
[PM] Which tabs are confirmed v1 scope for the User Performance section? Current design shows Users, Departments, Risk Scores, Training Progress, Time on Platform, Simulation History.
[Design] Wizard step bar ARIA annotation (DR-13) must be added before dev-handoff doc is written.
revision_priorities: Confirm DR-05 Reported signal scope with PM — design or defer before handoff
Add wizard step bar accessibility annotation (DR-13) — 10 minutes, required before running dev-handoff
Fix Save Changes CTA on Template Preview panel (DR-08) — replace with Close or Use Template
Replace Ransomware 4mins placeholder content (DR-14) with varied training card data
